corpus_version: Georgia SNAP Policy Manual MT-87, June 2026 (FY26 income limits effective Oct 1, 2025)
notes: Each scenario has an unambiguous expected outcome. Assertions are: exact enum match on eligibility_signal, must_cite_sections is an array of section numbers that MUST appear in applicable_rules[].section, must_include_deductions is section numbers that MUST appear in applicable_deductions[].section, special_notes_must_mention is a case-insensitive substring array, expedited_expected is boolean (must match how_to_apply.expedited_possible), and if refer_to_specialist_expected is true then signal MUST be refer_to_specialist. If a field is null the eval does not assert on it.

scenarios.tests: Baseline family case: household composition, earned income, dependent care, shelter deduction all apply.
scenarios.user_input: I'm a single mom in Atlanta with two kids, ages 4 and 7. I work at a daycare making about $1,900 a month before taxes. I pay $850 for rent and about $180 for electricity and water. I pay $400 a month for after-school care for my 7-year-old.

scenarios.tests: Gross income above 130% FPL for a household of 1. Tool should say ineligible on income but still encourage applying (deductions could change it if there's a wrinkle).
scenarios.user_input: I'm a single guy, 34, live alone, work full time as a delivery driver. I make $2,600 a month before taxes. I have about $500 in savings. My rent is $900 and utilities are around $150.

scenarios.tests: 60+ member: gross income test waived, only net test applies. Excess medical deduction should apply if any out-of-pocket costs mentioned.
scenarios.user_input: I'm 68 years old, retired, live by myself. I get $1,450 a month from Social Security and that's my only income. My rent is $700 and I pay about $110 for electricity and gas. I take a few prescriptions that cost me around $80 a month out of pocket.

scenarios.tests: Gross income right at the 130% FPL limit for household of 4 ($3,483). Should not confidently deny; deductions may still qualify them.
scenarios.user_input: Family of four, me and my wife and two kids under 10. I make about $3,400 a month working construction, my wife doesn't work right now. Rent is $1,200, utilities around $250. We have about $1,500 in the bank.

scenarios.tests: Self-employment income (3425). Net = gross receipts minus allowable business expenses.
scenarios.user_input: I do freelance graphic design out of my apartment. Last month I invoiced about $2,800 but I have around $900 in business expenses each month (software, contractor payments, phone). I live alone. Rent is $1,050, utilities around $130. About $800 in checking.

scenarios.tests: Student rule (3245): most 18-49 students in higher-ed are INELIGIBLE unless they meet an exemption. Input does not tell us whether the student meets any exemption. Correct behavior: tool asks a clarifying question OR commits to insufficient_info.
scenarios.user_input: I'm a 20-year-old college student. I live in an apartment near campus with two roommates but we cook separately. I have a small part-time job at the library that pays about $400 a month. My parents help with rent. Can I get SNAP?

scenarios.tests: Any mention of a non-citizen household member triggers 3320 hard block. Tool must NOT give a verdict on either the citizen kids OR the non-citizen adult.
scenarios.user_input: I'm a US citizen, my two kids were born here, but my husband is undocumented. He works construction under the table for about $2,000 a month cash. I don't work right now, I take care of the kids. Rent is $1,100. What can we get?

scenarios.tests: Excess medical deduction (3614) is a big deal for elderly/disabled: only medical expenses over $35/month count. Input has $220/month medical.
scenarios.user_input: I'm 74, my husband passed last year, I live alone in the house we owned. I get $1,600 a month from Social Security. My property tax and homeowners insurance work out to about $250 a month, plus $180 for utilities. I spend about $220 a month on prescriptions, a supplemental insurance premium, and rides to the doctor.

scenarios.tests: ABAWD (3355): single adult 18-52 with no dependents, no disability, not exempt. Must flag the 3-in-36 time limit and 80-hr work requirement in special_notes even though otherwise eligible.
scenarios.user_input: I'm 38, single, no kids, healthy. I got laid off from my warehouse job three months ago, been living off savings but that's almost gone. Down to about $80 in the account. I've been applying to jobs but nothing yet. Rent is $700, utilities around $100.

scenarios.tests: Homeless individual: 3618 (homeless shelter deduction) applies, expedited processing under 3110 clearly triggers. Signal: likely_eligible with expedited flag.
scenarios.user_input: I've been staying at a shelter downtown for the past six weeks. Before that I was on the street. I don't have a job right now. No money to speak of, maybe $5 in cash. No bank account.

scenarios.tests: 3210 categorical eligibility: households where all members receive TANF/SSI/TCOS are categorically eligible — meets gross AND net income limits automatically.
scenarios.user_input: It's just me and my 12-year-old daughter. She has autism and gets SSI, about $940 a month. I get SSI too because of a back injury, mine is about $860. That's all we live on. Rent is $600, utilities $120.

scenarios.tests: Expedited triggers ALL fire: gross < $150 + liquid < $100. Tool should also point to food bank in parallel (urgent-need routing).
scenarios.user_input: I lost my job last week and I have $40 to my name. Nothing else coming in. My kids and I haven't eaten a real meal in two days. I'm scared. Please help.
